key_insights.insight: Confidence can be rebuilt through slow, deliberate challenges, not necessarily adrenaline-fueled activities.
key_insights.quote: Loads of the things I do are really really slow you know like rowing across the atlantic took best part of two months walking across antarctica took many many months climbing everest took many months so actually if if it was jumping off a mountain base jumping or going uh on a motorbike or even a mountain bike down a steep slope. I hate all that. It's too fast.

key_insights.insight: Negative self-perception can be a self-fulfilling prophecy.
key_insights.quote: So much of what we do and what we endure and how we test ourselves is here in the mind and if you go in with a negative attitude which i had then it's selffulfilling.

key_insights.insight: Challenges can help to build confidence by forcing independent decision-making.
key_insights.quote: I had to kind of think on my own. I couldn't defer to other people... I didn't trust my own judgment. So first of all, that was gone. So I had to stand or fall on my own decisions.

key_insights.insight: A significant step towards self-belief and confidence lies in owning your unique personality and narrative.
key_insights.quote: Once you own your narrative, once you're confident that you are unique in whatever way... if you can own your personality, your narrative, and accept that, you're halfway there to this self-belief and this confidence.

key_insights.insight: The pressure to conform to societal expectations can be a significant source of internal doubt and insecurity.
key_insights.quote: I spent so much of my life trying to be the person I thought society wanted me to be. Always embarrassed that I wasn't, I was either too posh or I wasn't posh enough. I was either too successful or not successful enough.

key_insights.insight: High achievers are often burdened by a voice of self-doubt, which is a common experience, not an isolated one.
key_insights.quote: I know I'm not alone. I know there are many, many people out there who are high achievers, who've done brilliant things in life, but are still burdened with their own voice of doubt.

key_insights.insight: Confidence building isn't necessarily a conscious pursuit, but rather a gradual accumulation of experiences and challenges overcome.
key_insights.quote: It happened by accident. So that's the first thing to say. I didn't chase it thinking this is going to help. It was like a slow series of blocks that were built.

actionable_advice.advice: Embrace solo travel and living in a foreign country to foster independence.
actionable_advice.how_to: Plan a trip to a country with a different culture and language where you'll need to rely on your own decision-making without constant input from family and friends.

actionable_advice.advice: Consciously challenge negative self-talk and reframe perceived failures.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify specific instances of negative self-talk, question their validity, and replace them with more positive and realistic affirmations.

actionable_advice.advice: Actively work to 'own your narrative' by accepting and celebrating your unique qualities.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify the aspects of your personality that you might be suppressing due to social pressure, and find ways to express and celebrate those qualities in your daily life. Focus on 'being' rather than 'wanting' to be someone else.

memorable_stories: Guest's description of his difficult experience in the French school system due to undiagnosed dyslexia, which negatively impacted his self-confidence.
contrarian_claims: Adventure is not necessarily about adrenaline-fueled activities, but can involve slow, enduring challenges.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 18 - Ben Fogle on How He Lost & Found His Confidence
